exact.finite_descent: N=max(n0,N_m); prove n=N-1 down to n0
exact.fixed_order_tail: for every fixed m exists N_m such that for every n>=N_m and -100<=lambda<=0, Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0
exact.order10_delayed_handoff: [Q_(10,n)(-100)>0 for every integer n>=4] implies Q_(10,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0
exact.order11_delayed_handoff: [Q_(10,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0 and Q_(11,n)(-100)>0 for every n>=4] implies Q_(11,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0
exact.shifted_single_layer_implication: [Q_(m-1,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=n0 on -100<=lambda<=0, the fixed-order m eventual tail holds, and Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every n>=n0] => [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=n0 and -100<=lambda<=0]
exact.unused_prefix: the proof uses no Q_(m,n) with n<n0

proof_boundary: This proves a conditional propagation lemma and its order-ten and order-eleven n>=4 specializations. It does not prove either delayed endpoint premise, any sign for n=0,1,2,3 at negative lambda, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Every fixed signed Hankel order has a uniform positive heat tail.
rows.formula: for every fixed m exists N_m such that for every n>=N_m and -100<=lambda<=0, Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0
rows.proof_boundary: The threshold N_m may depend on m but not on lambda.

rows.claim: A positive lower layer makes the next-shift coupling strictly positive.
rows.proof_boundary: Requires Q_(m-1,n)>0 at the relevant shifts throughout the interval.

rows.claim: Variation of constants preserves strict positivity when the next shift is positive.
rows.proof_boundary: E is the positive integrating factor for the scalar equation at fixed n.

rows.claim: Choose N=max(n0,N_m) and descend from n=N-1 to n=n0; no smaller shift is used.
rows.proof_boundary: The descent has finitely many steps even though the shift chain is infinite.

rows.claim: Endpoint positivity on a delayed shift ray propagates on that same ray.
rows.formula: [Q_(m-1,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=n0 on -100<=lambda<=0, the fixed-order m eventual tail holds, and Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every n>=n0] => [Q_(m,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=n0 and -100<=lambda<=0]
rows.proof_boundary: No sign hypothesis or conclusion is made for n<n0.

rows.claim: The completed order-nine heat layer specializes the shifted lemma to m=10 and n0=4.
rows.formula: [Q_(10,n)(-100)>0 for every integer n>=4] implies Q_(10,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional only on the delayed endpoint premise at lambda=-100.

rows.claim: Once the shifted order-ten heat ray is available, the same descent specializes to m=11 and n0=4.
rows.formula: [Q_(10,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0 and Q_(11,n)(-100)>0 for every n>=4] implies Q_(11,n)(lambda)>0 for every n>=4 and -100<=lambda<=0
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional on both the shifted order-ten heat ray and the order-eleven endpoint ray.

sources.status: exact all-order endpoint-to-heat equivalence with a completed base through order nine and the candidate endpoint hierarchy rejected by the order-ten counterexample
exact order-ten positive endpoint-tail reduction with one open seventh-nested curvature ceiling

status: exact shifted cooperative heat-flow descent lemma
